spec_match.rationale: Compares the seven deprecated Intl.Locale getters to their getX() method form, exactly the spec deprecation surface.

interactive.rationale: Real <select> with 8 locales, live readout of all 7 properties under both forms; runs the API live in the visitor's browser.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises a side-by-side getter vs method demo; page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show what happens when the getter throws or returns undefined on engines without Intl Locale Info. Doesn't surface the DevTools deprecation warning the new release emits.

references.rationale: See also includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/5148228059398144 and the TC39 proposal link.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, deprecated card uses --accent-rose accent for the heading, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Surface the DevTools deprecation warning
openQuestions.detail: Chrome 131 emits a console deprecation warning when the getters are accessed. Capture console output (override console.warn) and pin it in the page so visitors see the warning live without opening DevTools.

openQuestions.title: Add an unsupported-engine fallback path
openQuestions.detail: If Intl.Locale.prototype.getCalendars doesn't exist (older engines), the demo should detect and show 'getter form only available on this engine'. Currently both columns will throw on those engines.

summary: Direct getter-vs-method comparison with live execution. Could surface the DevTools deprecation warning to drive the urgency home.
